title: err! macro calls .to_string() on format! result
description: The err! macro in lexer.rs (line 42) always calls .to_string() on its argument. When called as err!(format!(...)) — the only call site using a dynamic message (line 311) — this calls String::to_string() which is a redundant clone of an already-owned String. The macro should accept a String directly and assign it, not call .to_string(). String literals passed to the macro are fine to .to_owned(); the fix is to change .to_string() to .into() so both &str literals and String values are accepted without redundant allocation.
